info.title: Darkstone Cloud API
info.description: REST API for the Darkstone Cloud platform: container compute, virtual machines, encrypted volumes, S3-compatible object storage, the vector database, AI inference, the DDoS-protected Cloud Gateway, billing and project management. 🔐 Sign in with Google After a successful login the session cookie is automatically attached to every Swagger request (same-origin). For non-browser clients, set an API key via the **Authorize** button in the top right (header `X-API-Key`, format `ds_…`).

tags.name: VM Backups
tags.description: Incremental backups of the VM boot disk (self-service).

tags.name: Billing Config
tags.description: Stripe publishable key for the frontend init

tags.name: Cloud Gateways v2
tags.description: Host-independent public endpoints. v2 adds gateway tiers (shared/dedicated), free port/port-range selection on dedicated gateways, PROXY protocol v2 and protocol-less routes (always TCP+UDP).

tags.name: API Keys
tags.description: Manage the API keys of a project

tags.name: Credentials
tags.description: Credentials vault: API tokens, SSH keys, passwords and encryption keys with expiry dates.

tags.name: Tenant Networks
tags.description: Self-service virtuelle Netzwerke (VLAN/VPC) mit eigener VXLAN-VNI + IPAM. Feature-flag-gated (cloud.networks.enabled).

tags.name: Data-Plane: AI
tags.description: OpenAI-compatible invocation via the cloud gateway.

tags.name: Pricing
tags.description: Current price catalog of all cloud products (gross, EUR).

tags.name: Cloud Gateways
tags.description: Host-independent public endpoints for cloud resources. Routes bind TCP+UDP ports to specific resources (volume, bucket, collection, ...).

tags.name: Public Campaigns
tags.description: Anonymous lookups for the cookie banner

tags.name: Artificial Intelligence
tags.description: AIs deployed per project: text and image classifiers with embedded training data.

tags.name: Invitations
tags.description: Project invitations (shown in the UI as account notifications)

tags.name: Virtual Machines
tags.description: KubeVirt-based VMs with a Cloud Volume boot disk.

tags.name: Volume Snapshots
tags.description: Longhorn snapshot management for customer recovery use cases.

tags.name: Cloud Volumes
tags.description: Encrypted block volumes via Longhorn, with optional SFTP access through the gateway.

tags.name: Cloud Volumes — File Browser
tags.description: Web file browser for a Cloud Volume. No gateway needed; the forwarding layer talks internally to the volumes-sftp pod that has the PVCs mounted.

post.summary: Eine VM in das Netzwerk haengen (MOVE-Semantik)
post.description: Eine VM ist in HOECHSTENS einem Netz. Haengt die VM schon in einem anderen Netz, wird sie dorthin VERSCHOBEN (erst aus dem alten getrennt, dann ins neue gehaengt) — danach ist sie in genau diesem Netz. Idempotent, wenn sie schon hier haengt. Doppelter IDOR-Guard (Netz UND VM muessen zum Pfad-Projekt gehoeren). KubeVirt uebernimmt das geaenderte Secondary-Interface erst beim VMI-Neustart → restartRequired=true signalisiert 'pending restart'.

[DEPRECATED] Legacy Stripe-hosted checkout (redirect). The frontend uses /prepaid/payment-intent for embedded Elements.
Create a PaymentIntent for a Stripe Elements top-up. Returns the clientSecret with which the Stripe Elements form confirms the intent.
Server-side confirmation after the client confirm: books the balance + creates the invoice + saves the payment-method ID. Idempotent (Stripe invoice ID lookup).

Embedded checkout session (EWCS) for a prepaid top-up. The frontend mounts <EmbeddedCheckoutProvider clientSecret={...}> — Stripe handles all payment methods + 3DS automatically. The balance is booked via the checkout.session.completed webhook.
One-click top-up with the saved payment method (off-session). The frontend shows clientSecret only if Stripe requires 3DS.

Last-resort recovery: scans Stripe for succeeded SetupIntents/payment methods for the customer and saves the latest one on the billing account. Used when neither the webhook nor the direct confirm succeeded. Idempotent.
Embedded checkout session (EWCS) in mode=setup to add a card without charging. Counterpart to /payment-method/setup-intent for the new EWCS flow.

Bypass for the EWCS flow (mode=setup): after onComplete the frontend calls with the sessionId; the backend retrieves the session from Stripe, extracts the SetupIntent ID and saves the payment method. Idempotent.

post.summary: Deploy a new AI – optionally with a base model as the starting point. If baseModelLabels is set, only training entries with those labels are inherited from the base model (platform users may not see the contents, but they choose which classification classes they want).

post.summary: Add a training entry
Import training data from a CSV. Expected format: a 'label,payload' header row. For image classifiers, payload contains the base64-encoded byte stream of the image file.
Test an AI from the dashboard (rate-limited). Production calls go through the cloud gateways.

Atomically create a project — only AFTER a verified card. Creates the project + billing account + address + payment method and finalizes it (setupCompleted + starting credit) in one step. Nothing exists beforehand.
Embedded SETUP checkout (card-only) against the user-level Stripe customer — without a project.
Synchronously confirm the card after onComplete and save it on the user (independent of the webhook).
Attribute a campaign code from the affiliate cookie to the user
Decline an invitation (only the invited account email)
Accept an invitation (only the invited account email)
Stripe webhook endpoint

delete.summary: Delete a backup (blocks in the backup target are freed via garbage collection)

delete.description: Idempotent (war-nicht-dran == Erfolg). Das Secondary-Interface verschwindet erst beim VMI-Neustart → restartRequired spiegelt, ob sich etwas geaendert hat.
